title: Masao Tom Inada Oral History
creator: Tessaku
description: See this link for the most complete and recent information on this record: https://repository.californiarevealed.org/node/454982 .

Oral history interview with Masao Tom Inada, who was born in Sacramento and incarcerated as teenager in Tule Lake. After leaving Tule Lake early for an animation job in New York City, he was drafted into the Military Intelligence Service and served in Japan. Nearly 120,000 Japanese Americans were uprooted from their West Coast homes with the signing of Executive Order 9066 in February of 1942 and sent to inland concentration camps for four to five years.
